behavior_summary: This script implements a collection of item/NPC helpers (movement/path checks, animation dispatch, conversational entry/exit, idle wander and short monologues) plus a tiny wrapper that forwards hit/damage events to the core Item::receiveHit handler.

U8P_METHOD::receiveHitWrapper is a minimal entrypoint used by the engine when this object/NPC is struck; it forwards a fixed hit type (0x01) and the wrapper's arguments into Item::receiveHit so the engine's item damage logic handles the effect.

Conversation helpers (U8P_METHOD::beginConversation, U8P_METHOD::beginConversationNoMap, U8P_METHOD::endConversation, U8P_METHOD::checkConversationRange variants) coordinate UI ref resets, camera adjustments, avatar stasis, orientation/animation preparation, and wait-for-avatar-in-fast-area behavior so dialogues start and end cleanly.

Movement and path utilities (U8P_METHOD::func013E, U8P_METHOD::func03CD, U8P_METHOD::pathfindToItemByFrame, U8P_METHOD::func133F, U8P_METHOD::func143A) compute deltas, choose dominant axis, compute step counts and repeatedly call Item::legal_move / Item::move to validate or perform incremental steps — used for pathfinding, step validation, and teleport decisions.

Animation/turn/idle helpers (U8P_METHOD::func15E7, U8P_METHOD::func091C, U8P_METHOD::npcIdleWander, U8P_METHOD::reverseAnimFrame) manage facing, play Npc::doAnim with specific parameters, and implement idle wandering including randomized choices.

Dialogue/monologue utilities (U8P_METHOD::func00A9, U8P_METHOD::barkAndWait, U8P_METHOD::barkIfNotBusy, U8P_METHOD::func0676) present menus, emit barks with blocking waits, and produce short scripted effects (SFX, spawn body-part effect, and optionally apply receiveHit damage) as part of NPC behavior.
notable_control_flow: U8P_METHOD::receiveHitWrapper: single-step forwarder with no branching — ensures damage events are handled by Item::receiveHit with a canonical hit type (0x01).
Menu/ask loop (U8P_METHOD::func00A9): uses a suspend/Ask and a small retry counter (local3) compared to param2 to limit attempts before returning the captured selection.

Conversation startup (U8P_METHOD::beginConversation / U8P_METHOD::beginConversationNoMap): resets UI refs, optionally freezes the avatar (setAvatarInStasis), spawns orientation/animation helper (U8P_METHOD::func091C), plays randomized bark sequences depending on item status bits, and waits for avatar proximity (Item::enterFastArea) before finalizing.

Movement/path helpers (U8P_METHOD::func013E / U8P_METHOD::func03CD): compute dx/dy, pick a dominant axis using absolute-value comparisons, produce division/modulo-derived step distributions, possibly apply vertical/frame alignment logic, then iterate simulated steps calling Item::legal_move to determine move legality.

Animation stepping (U8P_METHOD::func091C): repeatedly adjusts Npc::getDir toward a target direction in small increments with suspend points and an attempt counter that can probabilistically abort further incremental turning.

Conversation teardown (U8P_METHOD::endConversation): waits for avatar entering fast-area, then chooses a short cleanup/transition spawn based on distance, clears somebodyTalking and un-stases the avatar.
Loop/frame progression inside U8P_METHOD::func00A9; treated as control-flow, not explicit internal call.
open_questions: Exact numeric meanings of several numeric constants used in frame/vertical alignment math (for example the divisor 0x14 in U8P_METHOD::func013E) — inferred as frame/height granularity but not certain.

Precise semantic of param3/param4 in U8P_METHOD::func013E/U8P_METHOD::func03CD: they are used for z/frame comparisons and sign/multiplier flipping but their calling contract (units and callers) is not fully explicit.

Which global or script-level flags beyond somebodyTalking and avatar stasis are relied upon across broader game logic (some flags are read/written locally but global flag table in metadata is sparse).

Whether some of the Unknown nodes represent subtle side effects (special-case spawns or implicit resource references) that the decompiler could not structure — these could affect corner behavior of movement or conversation.

main_function.name: U8P_METHOD::receiveHitWrapper
main_function.purpose: Expose an engine-visible receive-hit entry that forwards the hit to the core item damage handler (Item::receiveHit) with a standardized hit type (0x01) and the wrapper's parameters.
entry_conditions: Called when the engine registers a hit on this object/NPC (damage event).
Invoked by the runtime as the object's receive-hit entrypoint (implicit 'this' receiver).

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::receiveHitWrapper
function_map.role: damage-event entrypoint / forwarder to Item::receiveHit

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::getDirToCoordsWrapper
function_map.role: adapter that calls Item::getDirToCoords with reordered args

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::func00A9
function_map.role: present an Ask menu, suspend until player selection, return selection (retry-limited)

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::toggleStatusFlag
function_map.role: flip specified bits in Item::getStatus via Item::andStatus/Item::orStatus

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::func013E
function_map.role: movement/step validation utility (delta math, step distribution and Item::legal_move calls)

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::func03CD
function_map.role: alternate movement/step validation utility similar to U8P_METHOD::func013E

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::reverseAnimFrame
function_map.role: adjust animation frame backward by a small delta to affect sprite sequencing

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::func0676
function_map.role: short monologue/effect: spawn SFX/sprite, optional bark and spawn, optionally destroy and apply damage via Item::receiveHit

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::barkAndWait
function_map.role: emit a bark/dialogue line and wait synchronously for completion

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::barkIfNotBusy
function_map.role: guarded bark that only plays if the NPC/item is not busy

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::func091C
function_map.role: direction/turn helper that steps NPC facing toward a target and dispatches animations

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::checkConversationRange
function_map.role: timed loop that advances a small animation counter and spawns Npc::doAnim to keep NPC in pre-conversation state

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::npcIdleWander
function_map.role: idle wander/behavior dispatcher that chooses random idle animations and occasional rotation

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::beginConversation
function_map.role: conversation startup: UI cleanup, avatar stasis, orientation, barks, camera handling and waiting for avatar proximity

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::beginConversationNoMap
function_map.role: U8P_METHOD::beginConversation variant that omits map-specific behavior but performs the same conversation prep

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::endConversation
function_map.role: conversation teardown: wait for avatar enter-fast-area, spawn cleanup/transition, clear somebodyTalking and un-stasis avatar

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::getWeaponDirFromFrame
function_map.role: map animation frame ranges to weapon-facing/direction codes

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::getNpcSocialGroup
function_map.role: classify the NPC/item into a social group code used by higher logic

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::checkConversationRangeAlt
function_map.role: alternate conversation-range sequence; runs three animation dispatches and optionally stases avatar

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::func133F
function_map.role: teleport decision & execution helper (distance/map checks, then Npc::teleport when warranted)

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::func143A
function_map.role: proximity + map-check helper returning whether a target is far/different-map

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::pathfindToItemByFrame
function_map.role: iterate items by frame id and spawn Npc::pathfind to located frame-marked items (looped with retries)

function_map.name: U8P_METHOD::func15E7
function_map.role: animation dispatcher that resets a ref and spawns Npc::doAnim(anim=0x00,duration=0x2710,param2,param1)

internal_calls.purpose: play an animation or animation-step while stepping NPC facing; used between suspend points
spawn orientation/turn helper so NPC faces player before dialog
play opening barks and wait for them to complete during conversation startup
rotate NPC toward a randomly chosen direction when wandering
trigger idle animation(s) each wander cycle
used for pacing/animation around pathfinding attempts (suspend/animation loop)

external_calls.purpose: deliver the damage/hit event to the item's canonical handler with hit type 0x01 and forwarded parameters
compute facing/direction from this item to a target coordinate
read current X for delta/step calculations
validate each simulated incremental step for collision/terrain legality
spawn/play an NPC animation (anim id 0x00, duration 0x2710) with forwarded parameters
spawn a short helper process used as a timed wait or to manage bark playback, then suspend on it
clear UI/process references and close UI gumps before/after conversation start
spawn a camera-follow helper when avatar is far to adjust camera for conversation
wait for the avatar to enter the NPC's fast-area trigger as part of conversation teardown synchronization
apply damage/hit to a target after playing visual/sound effects and randomizing damage

reads.name: avatarInStasis
reads.likely_effect: checked to decide whether to set avatar stasis during conversation startup

reads.name: Item status bit 0x10
reads.likely_effect: read to choose alternate bark sequences in U8P_METHOD::beginConversation/NoMap

reads.name: devonInRule
reads.likely_effect: used in U8P_METHOD::getNpcSocialGroup to select a particular social group mapping in conjunction with 'this' == 0x02

writes.name: somebodyTalking
writes.likely_effect: set to 1 at conversation start to mark conversation state; cleared by U8P_METHOD::endConversation during teardown

writes.name: avatarInStasis
writes.likely_effect: set to 1 to freeze the avatar for conversation prep and later cleared to 0 to restore control

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements a collection of item/NPC helpers (movement/path checks, animation dispatch, conversational entry/exit, idle wander and short monologues) plus a tiny wrapper that forwards hit/damage events to the core Item::receiveHit handler.
